_meta.description: All core playable races with ability adjustments, requirements, special abilities, and class restrictions

races.name: Human
races.description: The most common and versatile race, humans have no ability score adjustments or racial restrictions on class selection.
special_abilities: May dual-class: switch to a new class, retaining old class abilities once new class exceeds old class level
No level limits in any class
Access to all character classes including Paladin and Ranger
Unlimited exceptional STR (18/xx) only if Fighter class
races.notes: Humans are the only race able to dual-class.

races.name: Dwarf
races.description: Stout and resilient demihumans known for their affinity with stone, metals, and deep places.
special_abilities: Attack bonus: 1 to attack rolls vs. orcs, half-orcs, goblins, and hobgoblins
Saving throw bonus: 1 to saves for every 3.5 CON points (vs. magic, rods, staves, wands, and poison)
Dodge bonus: -4 to attack rolls of ogres, trolls, ogre magi, giants, and titans targeting the dwarf
Detect grade/slope in passage: 75% chance
Detect new tunnel/passage construction: 75% chance
Detect sliding/shifting walls or rooms: 66% chance
Detect stonework traps, pits, deadfalls: 50% chance
Determine approximate depth underground: 50% chance
Saving throw bonus vs. magic: CON-based (see notes)

saving_throw_bonuses.description: Dwarves gain saving throw bonuses based on CON score

races.notes: Dwarves have a naturally low movement rate of 6 but are not slowed by encumbrance as heavily as other races.

races.name: Elf
races.description: Graceful and long-lived demihumans with keen senses and innate magical talents.
special_abilities: 90% resistance to sleep and charm spells
Detect secret doors: 1-in-6 passing within 10 ft (passive), 2-in-6 when actively searching
Detect concealed doors: 3-in-6 when searching
+1 to attack rolls with long swords, short swords, and bows
Move silently in non-metal armor: surprise opponents on 4-in-6 chance
Infravision 60 ft

saving_throw_bonuses.description: +90% resistance to sleep and charm spells (these saves are rarely failed)

races.notes: Elves who actively search for secret doors have a 2-in-6 chance of finding them.

races.name: Gnome
races.description: Small, inventive demihumans related to dwarves, with a deep love of gems, illusions, and mechanisms.
special_abilities: Attack bonus: 1 to attack rolls vs. kobolds and goblins
Dodge bonus: -4 to attack rolls of gnolls, bugbears, ogres, trolls, ogre magi, giants, and titans targeting the gnome
Saving throw bonus vs. magic: CON-based (same as dwarves)
Detect grade/slope in passage: 80% chance
Detect unsafe walls, ceilings, floors: 70% chance
Determine approximate depth underground: 60% chance
Determine approximate direction underground: 50% chance
Speak with burrowing mammals (badgers, moles, etc.)

saving_throw_bonuses.description: Gnomes gain saving throw bonuses based on CON score (same table as Dwarves)

races.notes: Gnomes can speak with burrowing mammals as a natural ability.

races.name: Half-Elf
races.description: The offspring of humans and elves, blending the best of both races with some limitations of each.
special_abilities: 30% resistance to sleep and charm spells
Detect secret doors: 1-in-6 passing within 10 ft (passive), 2-in-6 when actively searching
Detect concealed doors: 3-in-6 when searching
Infravision 60 ft
races.notes: Half-elves have the widest variety of available multi-class combinations.

races.name: Halfling
races.description: Small, cheerful demihumans known for their stealth, luck, and love of comfortable homes.
special_abilities: Attack bonus: 3 to attack rolls with thrown weapons and slings
Saving throw bonus: 1 for every 3.5 CON points vs. magic, rods, staves, wands, and poison (same as Dwarves)
Dodge bonus: -4 to attack rolls of ogres, trolls, ogre magi, giants, and titans targeting the halfling
Move silently outdoors: surprise enemies on 4-in-6 chance
Near-invisibility when motionless in natural surroundings
Stout subraces: infravision 60 ft, underground abilities like Dwarves
Tallfellow subraces: similar to Elves for secret/concealed door detection

saving_throw_bonuses.description: Halflings gain saving throw bonuses based on CON (same table as Dwarves)

races.notes: Halflings have excellent attack bonuses with thrown weapons and slings. Stout halflings gain dwarf-like underground abilities.

races.name: Half-Orc
races.description: Part human and part orc, these beings are often strong and tough but lack social grace.
special_abilities: Infravision 60 ft
races.notes: Half-orcs have the fewest special racial abilities but strong STR/CON. Assassin class (if used) has no level limit for half-orcs. Note: Assassin class is optional in 2e.

multiclass_rules.description: Demihumans (non-humans) may choose multi-class combinations, splitting XP and advancing in two or three classes simultaneously.
multiclass_rules.xp_split: XP earned is divided equally among all classes
multiclass_rules.hp_calculation: Roll hit dice for each class and average the results (round fractions down, minimum 1)
multiclass_rules.class_abilities: Character has access to all class abilities from all active classes, subject to restrictions
multiclass_rules.armor_restrictions: Must follow the most restrictive armor rules of all classes taken
restrictions: Only demihumans may multi-class; humans may dual-class instead
Cannot multi-class two classes from the same group (two Warrior classes, etc.)
Thieving skills cannot be used in armor not permitted by the thief class
Clerics in multi-class using cleric spells must follow standard cleric weapon restrictions

dualclass_rules.description: Humans only. A character stops advancing in their original class and begins a new one.
requirements: Score of 15+ in the prime requisite(s) of the original class
Score of 17+ in the prime requisite(s) of the new class
dualclass_rules.regaining_old_class: When the new class level exceeds the old class level, the character regains all abilities of the original class, except when wearing armor or using weapons prohibited by the new class.
dualclass_rules.xp: All XP goes to the new class; original class level is frozen
